@graph.headline: Mastering Kaizen: Why Your Tech Team Needs a Continuous Improvement Manager
@graph.description: How replacing rigid data with intuitive signals reduced burnout and stabilized a high-velocity sales team during the AT&T/DIRECTV merger.

acceptedAnswer.text: Kaizen is a Japanese business philosophy that translates to 'continuous improvement.' It involves all employees, from the CEO to the assembly line workers, and focuses on eliminating waste and improving processes through small, incremental steps.

mainEntity.name: Why did replacing numbers with colors improve sales performance?

acceptedAnswer.text: Raw data often creates 'cognitive overload' and anxiety, especially during a high-stress transition like a corporate merger. By replacing complex numbers with simple color indicators, the salespeople were able to immediately understand their standing without feeling micromanaged.

mainEntity.name: How does Kaizen relate to AI and technology?

acceptedAnswer.text: Before you can automate a process with AI, you must first simplify it. If you apply AI to a broken, overly complex process, you just get automated chaos. Kaizen is the discipline of aligning the people and simplifying the process before the technology is deployed.
